description: FIRE Retirement Planner — risk-adjusted, tax-minimised Monte Carlo over jurisdictions, withdrawal strategies, and UK-departure years. Backed by fire_planner schema on pg-cluster-rw.

list.name: scenario
list.label: Scenario
list.query: SELECT external_id FROM fire_planner.scenario ORDER BY external_id

targets.rawSql: SELECT snapshot_date AS time, account_name AS metric, SUM(market_value_gbp) AS value FROM fire_planner.account_snapshot WHERE snapshot_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '10 years' GROUP BY snapshot_date, account_name ORDER BY snapshot_date

panels.title: Monte Carlo fan chart — selected scenario
panels.description: P10/p25/p50/p75/p90 portfolio value across MC paths, for the scenario picked in the selector at the top.

targets.rawSql: SELECT (DATE_TRUNC('year', NOW()) + (year_idx || ' years')::interval) AS time, 'p10' AS metric, p10_portfolio_gbp AS value FROM fire_planner.projection_yearly p JOIN fire_planner.mc_run r ON r.id = p.mc_run_id JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE s.external_id = '$scenario' UNION ALL SELECT (DATE_TRUNC('year', NOW()) + (year_idx || ' years')::interval), 'p25', p25_portfolio_gbp FROM fire_planner.projection_yearly p JOIN fire_planner.mc_run r ON r.id = p.mc_run_id JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE s.external_id = '$scenario' UNION ALL SELECT (DATE_TRUNC('year', NOW()) + (year_idx || ' years')::interval), 'p50', p50_portfolio_gbp FROM fire_planner.projection_yearly p JOIN fire_planner.mc_run r ON r.id = p.mc_run_id JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE s.external_id = '$scenario' UNION ALL SELECT (DATE_TRUNC('year', NOW()) + (year_idx || ' years')::interval), 'p75', p75_portfolio_gbp FROM fire_planner.projection_yearly p JOIN fire_planner.mc_run r ON r.id = p.mc_run_id JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE s.external_id = '$scenario' UNION ALL SELECT (DATE_TRUNC('year', NOW()) + (year_idx || ' years')::interval), 'p90', p90_portfolio_gbp FROM fire_planner.projection_yearly p JOIN fire_planner.mc_run r ON r.id = p.mc_run_id JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE s.external_id = '$scenario' ORDER BY time

panels.title: Confidence heatmap — jurisdiction × strategy
panels.description: Median success rate by (jurisdiction, strategy), averaged across leave-UK years and glide paths.

targets.rawSql: SELECT jurisdiction, strategy, AVG(success_rate) AS avg_success FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary GROUP BY jurisdiction, strategy ORDER BY jurisdiction, strategy
SELECT jurisdiction, AVG(median_lifetime_tax_gbp) AS lifetime_tax FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary GROUP BY jurisdiction ORDER BY lifetime_tax DESC

panels.title: Withdrawal runway — years to ruin (failing paths)
panels.description: Among scenarios where some MC paths failed, the median year-to-ruin. Empty where every path survives.

targets.rawSql: SELECT jurisdiction, strategy, leave_uk_year, glide_path, median_years_to_ruin FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary WHERE median_years_to_ruin IS NOT NULL ORDER BY median_years_to_ruin ASC LIMIT 20

panels.title: Optimal leave-UK year
panels.description: leave_uk_year that maximises success_rate − lifetime_tax (tax in £M; small weighting).

targets.rawSql: SELECT leave_uk_year FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary WHERE jurisdiction <> 'uk' ORDER BY (success_rate - median_lifetime_tax_gbp / 1000000.0) DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT p50_ending_gbp FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary WHERE scenario_id = (SELECT id FROM fire_planner.scenario WHERE external_id = '$scenario')

panels.title: Success rate vs spend (UK-stay)
panels.description: Sanity gauge — UK success rate by strategy, helps anchor expectations against published cFIREsim numbers.

targets.rawSql: SELECT strategy, AVG(success_rate) AS success FROM fire_planner.scenario_summary WHERE jurisdiction = 'uk' GROUP BY strategy ORDER BY success DESC

panels.title: Sequence-of-returns sensitivity (top failing scenarios)
panels.description: Pearson correlation between year-1 portfolio drawdown and overall success — strongly negative ⇒ scenario is sequence-of-returns sensitive (case for the rising-equity glide).

targets.rawSql: SELECT s.external_id, r.sequence_risk_correlation, r.success_rate FROM fire_planner.mc_run r JOIN fire_planner.scenario s ON s.id = r.scenario_id WHERE r.id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM fire_planner.mc_run GROUP BY scenario_id) ORDER BY r.sequence_risk_correlation ASC LIMIT 15

title: FIRE Planner
